LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN CHAN AS TRUSTEE OF THE SHU PUN CHAN TRUST'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 27, 2013 AT 9:30 A.M. FOR AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR THE DEFENDANT'S FORMER COUNSEL FROM NIXON PEABODY LLP TO SHOW CAUSE WHY SANCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED AGAINST THEM OR THE DEFENDANTS PURSUANT TO CCP 2023.030; CCP 2030.300(D); CCP 2031.300(H) AND CCP 2033.290(D). DEFENDANTS FORMER COUNSEL MAY FILE A BRIEF IN CONNECTION WITH THE MOTION FOR SANCTION, 9 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. REPLY BRIEFS MAY BE FILED, 5 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. IF THE PARTIES REACH A RESOLUTION OF THE ISSUE OF SANCTIONS WITHOUT FURTHER NECESSITY OF THE COURT, THEY MAY CONTACT THE CLERK OF DEPT. 302 IN WRITING AND MAY BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

Order To Show Cause Why Sanction Should Not Be Issued Against Defendants
Off Calendar Mar-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling, which was not contested, on the Order To Show Cause Why Sanction Should Not Be Issued Against Defendants, as follows: The Court has read the supplemental briefs filed by former counsel for Defendants and current counsel for Defendants in light of the motion to compel discovery filed November 2, 2012. The Court withdraws the order to show cause and will not award attorney fees in connection with the motion to compel. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 (After hearing, the court signed the proposed order that was emailed to the court.)

Order To Show Cause Why Sanction Should Not Be Issued Against Defendants
Off Calendar Feb-27-2013 Continued To Mar-19-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Order To Show Cause Why Sanction Should Not Be Issued Against Defendants is continued on the court's own motion to March 19, 2013 to give the parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The parties must deliver courtesy copies of their supplemental papers filed on February 13, 2013 and February 15, 2013 to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than February 27, 2013 at 2:00 pm. The Court has a courtesy copy of the Declaration of Loppato, filed February 13, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-17-2013 Continued To Apr-29-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JORDAN A. SUSSMAN OF ZIEF, DIMENT & GLICKMAN, TEL. NO. 986-3644 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL SHKLOVSKY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF MATTANIAH EYTAN, TEL. NO. 399-1000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - PLAINTIFF JOHN CHAN AS TRUSTEE OF THE SHU PUN CHAN TRUST'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions is continued to February 27, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. for an Order to show cause for the defendant's former counsel from Nixon Peabody LLP to show cause why sanction should not be issued against them or the defendants pursuant to CCP 2023.030; CCP 2030.300(d); CCP 2031.300(h) AND CCP 2033.290(d). Defendants former counsel may file a brief in connection with the motion for sanction, 9 court days before the hearing. Reply briefs may be filed, 5 court days before the hearing. If the parties reach a resolution of the issue of sanctions without further necessity of the court, they may contact the clerk of Dept. 302 in writing and may be taken off calendar. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. = (302/MJM)

Jordan A. Sussman, Zief & Glickman, 415.986.3644, appearing for the Plaintiff;
